reference: I am a type one diabetic and I have had severe chronic bladder infections with inflammation of my left kidney. My kidney was so inflamed at one point that I could not button my pants. I was hospitalized recently and was told the day I was discharged that I was near kidney failure when I arrived. Now, when I start hurting in my lower back, I can't even massage or touch the area because it hurts to touch it. Should I be more concerned about this, or is there anything I can do to help myself? I can't afford to see a doctor right now!
text: I am a type 1 diabetic and I have had severe chronic bladder infections with inflammation of my left kidney. My kidney was so inflamed at one point that I could not button my pants. I was hospitalized recently and was told the day I was discharged that I was near kidney failure when I arrived. Now, when I start hurting in my lower back, I can't even massage or touch the area because it hurts to touch it. Should I be more concerned about this, or is there anything I can do to help myself?
